paper.title: Dissecting the Impact of Mobile DVFS Governors on LLM Inference Performance and Energy Efficiency
paper.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being integrated into various applications and services running on billions of mobile devices. However, deploying LLMs on resource-limited mobile devices faces a significant challenge due to their high demand for computation, memory, and ultimately energy. While current LLM frameworks for mobile use three power-hungry components-CPU, GPU, and Memory-even when running primarily-GPU LLM models, optimized DVFS governors for CPU, GPU, and memory featured

memory_insight.memory_bandwidth_requirements: up to 40.4% longer prefilling and decoding latency
memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: up to 40.4% longer prefilling and decoding latency

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Optimizing the energy efficiency of LLM inference on mobile devices requires a unified energy-aware governor that considers the intricate interplay among CPU, GPU, and memory frequencies.
technical_details: DVFS governors for CPU, GPU, and memory
Optimized combinations of CPU, GPU, and memory frequencies
LLM inference performance and energy efficiency
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on mobile devices with 4-8GB RAM, as it reduces time-to-first-token and time-per-output-token latencies by 7.0%-16.9% and 25.4%-36.8% on average with the same energy-per-token for various mobile LLM models.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
